hints.text: Self-governing **Channel Island** off NW France; British Crown Dependency — **counts as UK** in streaks
All coverage = **Gen2**; English-style winding hedgerow/tree-tunnel lanes but usually **sunny weather**
hints.uniq_note: Sunny Gen2 'England' with tree tunnels = Jersey lean; mainland UK Gen2 is rarer and usually gloomier.

shared_with.note: Isle of Man is also all-Gen2 with a similar British look — but gloomier and hillier.
shared_with.diff: Sunny + French names → Jersey; gloomy/hilly + red-strip plates → Isle of Man.

hints.text: Plates UK-model (white front / **yellow rear**) + the island's **red coat of arms** left + **'J' prefix**; **no blue strip** (Jersey was never in the EU)
hints.uniq_note: The red arms + J-prefix are Jersey-only; blurred, it still reads as a UK plate.

shared_with.note: Same white/yellow model; mainland plates (post-Brexit aside) often had a blue strip.
shared_with.diff: Red coat of arms + J → Jersey.

hints.text: Road infrastructure like the UK: **drives left**, UK-style directional signs, chevrons, warning signs

shared_with.diff: French names + yellow give-way lines + sunny Gen2 → Jersey.

shared_with.note: Same UK-style kit on the Isle of Man.
shared_with.diff: French locality names → Jersey; Manx names (Balla-) → IoM.

hints.text: Vast majority of street/locality names contain **French words** (traditional language **Jèrriais** on some signs)
hints.uniq_note: French names + left-hand traffic + UK signage = Channel Islands; the J-plates then confirm Jersey.

hints.text: Architecture like northern France: **pastel walls + dark slate roofs**; British terraced houses also common; many walls/houses of local **red granite**
**Cornish palms** (thin trunks, arching leaves) common as decoration

shared_with.note: Also on some mainland coastal stretches.

hints.text: **Yellow give-way lines** at intersections (UK uses double dotted white)
hints.uniq_note: Within the UK-signage world the yellow give-way line is the fastest Jersey tell.

shared_with.note: Mainland UK uses double dotted WHITE lines for the same give-way purpose.
shared_with.diff: Yellow → Jersey; white dots → UK mainland.

hints.text: Extensive cycling network: **'Green Lane' signs** + blue route markers w/ an island map in the centre; some signs asymmetrically attached to posts (more than mainland)
Most street signs carry the **coat of arms of their parish** — enlargeable overview graphic

**St Helier** area = urbanised (taller buildings, narrow lively streets); **Les Mielles** reserve = vast treeless western coast w/ gentle westward slope + partially overcast coverage; SW of the island: **greenish-purple glitch** imagery (darker screen; not Jersey-exclusive)

**Spotlights**: airport boundary fence; **La Collette power-station chimney** visible along the south coast (pinpoint anchor); **Victoria Ave** west of St Helier = the only divided road, w/ a maroon median
